author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

ccel.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm XXXIII

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Rejoice in the Lord, O ye righteous:” rejoice, O ye righteous, not in yourselves, for that is not safe; but in the Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For praise is comely to the upright” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 1): these praise the Lord, who submit themselves unto the Lord; for else they are distorted and perverse.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Praise the Lord with harp:” praise the Lord, presenting unto Him your bodies a living sacrifice.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Sing unto Him with the psaltery for ten strings” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 2): let your members be servants to the love of God, and of your neighbour, in which are kept both the three and the seven commandments.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Sing unto Him a new song:” sing unto Him a song of the grace of faith.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Sing skilfully unto Him with jubilation” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 3): sing skillfully unto Him with rejoicing.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For the Word of the Lord is right:” for the Word of the Lord is right, to make you that which of yourselves ye cannot be.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And all His works are done in faith” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 4): lest any think that by the merit of works he hath arrived at faith, when in faith are done all the works which God Himself loveth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He loveth Mercy and Judgment:” for He loveth Mercy, which now He showeth first; and Judgment, wherewith He exacteth that which He hath first shown.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The earth is full of the Mercy of the Lord” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 5): throughout the whole world are sins forgiven unto men by the Mercy of the Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “By the Word of the Lord were the heavens made firm:” for not by themselves, but by the Word of the Lord were the righteous made strong.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And all the strength of them by the Breath of His Mouth” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And all their faith by His Holy Spirit.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He gathereth the waters of the sea together as into a bottle:” He gathered the people of the world together, to confession of mortified sin, lest through pride they flow too freely.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He layeth up the deep in storehouses” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 7): and keepeth in them His secrets for riches.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let all the earth fear the Lord:” let every sinner fear, that so he may cease to sin.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let all the inhabitants of the world stand in awe of Him” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 8): not of the terrors of men, or of any creature, but of Him let them stand in awe.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For He spake, and they were made:” for no other one made those things which are to fear; but He spake, and they were made.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He commanded, and they were created” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 9): He commanded by His Word, and they were created.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The Lord bringeth the counsel of the heathen to nought;” of them that seek not His Kingdom, but kingdoms of their own.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He maketh the devices of the people of none effect:” of them that covet earthly happiness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And reproveth the counsels of princes” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 10): of them that seek to rule over such peoples.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “But the counsel of the Lord standeth for ever;” but the counsel of the Lord, whereby He maketh none blessed but him that submitteth unto Himself, standeth for ever.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The thoughts of His Heart to all generations” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 11): the thoughts of His Wisdom are not mutable, but endure to all generations.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Blessed is the nation whose God is the Lord:” one nation is blessed, belonging to the heavenly city, which hath not chosen save the Lord for their God: “And the people whom He hath chosen for His own inheritance” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 12): and which not of itself, but by the gift of God, hath been chosen, that He by possessing it may not suffer it to be uncared for and miserable.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The Lord looketh from Heaven; He beholdeth all the sons of men” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: From the souls of the righteous, the Lord looketh mercifully upon all who would rise to newness of life.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “From His prepared habitation:” from His habitation of assumed Humanity, which He prepared for Himself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He looketh upon all the inhabitants of the earth” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 14): He looketh mercifully upon all who live in the flesh, that He may be over them in ruling them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He fashioneth their hearts singly:” He giveth spiritually to their hearts their proper gifts, so that neither the whole body may be eye, nor the whole hearing; but that one in this manner, another in that manner, may be incorporated with Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He understandeth all their works” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Before Him are all their works understood.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “A king shall not be saved by much strength:” he shall not be saved who ruleth his own flesh, if he presume much upon his own strength.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Neither shall a giant be saved by much strength” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 16): nor shall he be saved whoever warreth against the habit of his own lust, or against the devil and his angels, if he trust much to his own might.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “A horse is a deceitful thing for safety:” he is deceived, who thinketh either that through men he gaineth salvation received among men, or that by the impetuosity of his own courage he is defended from destruction.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “In the abundance of his strength shall he not be saved” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Behold, the Eyes of the Lord are upon them that fear Him:” because if thou seek salvation, behold, the love of the Lord is upon them that fear Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Upon them that hope in His mercy” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 18): that hope not in their own strength, but in His mercy.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “To deliver their souls from death, and to keep them alive in famine” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To give them the nourishment of the Word, and of Everlasting Truth, which they lost while presuming on their own strength, and therefore have not even their own strength, from lack of righteousness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “My soul shall be patient for the Lord:” that hereafter it may be filled with dainties incorruptible, meanwhile, whilst here it remaineth, my soul shall be patient for the Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For He is our Helper and Defender” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 20): our Helper He is, while we endeavour after Him; and our Defender, while we resist the adversary.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For our heart shall rejoice in Him:” for not in ourselves, wherein without Him there is great need; but in Himself shall our heart rejoice.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And we have trusted in His holy Name” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 21); and therefore have we trusted that we shall come to God, because unto us absent hath He sent, through faith, His own Name.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let Thy mercy, O Lord, be upon us, according as we have hoped in Thee” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 22): let Thy mercy, O Lord, be upon us; for hope confoundeth not, because we have hoped in Thee.
